name: Creator Design System / Accessibility / WCAG Compliance
description: WCAG 2.2 rules and page-by-page audit checklists for every CDS foundation. Tied to actual token values across the design-system folder.

meta.subline: Every token, component, and layout in CDS must clear WCAG 2.2 AA. AAA where it doesn't compromise the brand.

perceivable.label: Perceivable
perceivable.description: Information and UI components must be presentable to users in ways they can perceive.

operable.label: Operable
operable.description: UI components and navigation must be operable.

understandable.label: Understandable
understandable.description: Information and UI operation must be understandable.

robust.label: Robust
robust.description: Content must be robust enough to be interpreted by user agents, including assistive technologies.

uiComponents.appliesTo: Borders, icons, focus rings, form fields

incidental.appliesTo: Disabled, decorative, or pure-decoration text — exempt

aaa.note: Aim for AAA on long-form reading surfaces.

focusVisible.name: Focus Visible
focusVisible.rule: Every interactive element must show a clearly visible focus indicator.

focusNotObscured.name: Focus Not Obscured (Minimum)
focusNotObscured.rule: When an element has focus, it must not be entirely hidden by other content.

targetSize.name: Target Size (Minimum)
targetSize.rule: Touch targets must be at least 24×24 CSS pixels.

textSpacing.name: Text Spacing
textSpacing.rule: Layouts must not break when users override these values:

resize.name: Resize Text
resize.rule: Text must be resizable up to 200% without loss of content or functionality.
resize.implication: Use rem/em units for type, not fixed px.

reflow.name: Reflow
reflow.rule: Content must reflow without horizontal scrolling at 320 CSS pixels wide.
reflow.implication: Mobile breakpoint must work down to 320px; nothing should require horizontal scroll except tables, maps, code.

nonTextContrast.name: Non-Text Contrast
nonTextContrast.rule: UI components and graphical objects must have at least 3:1 contrast against adjacent colors.

nameRoleValue.name: Name, Role, Value
nameRoleValue.rule: Every UI component must have a programmatic name, role, and current state exposed to assistive tech.
nameRoleValue.implication: Every CTA needs a meaningful accessible name; icon-only buttons need aria-label.

useOfColor.name: Use of Color
useOfColor.rule: Color must not be the only means of conveying information.
useOfColor.implication: Errors need an icon or text label, not just red.

motion.name: Animation from Interactions
motion.rule: Motion animation triggered by interaction can be disabled.

items.check: Default body line-height ≥ 1.5× font size — verify space-md-1 (24px) accommodates 16px text comfortably.
Minimum tappable space between adjacent CTAs ≥ 8px (preferably 12-16px).
items.evidence: sm-2 (8px) is the floor; sm-3 (12px) recommended.

items.check: Section gaps must collapse gracefully on mobile.
items.evidence: Spaces tokens are static; consumers need responsive rules (e.g., xl-10 → md-4 on XXS).

items.check: Form field vertical padding ≥ sm-3 (12px) so 24px touch target is met.
Layout reflows without horizontal scroll at 320px viewport.
items.evidence: XXS breakpoint is 576px — verify there are layout rules below 576px.

items.check: Grid gutters scale with text resize.
items.evidence: Gutters max at 32px (ML), drop to 16px on small breakpoints.

items.check: Column count reduces sensibly: 12 → 8 → 4.
items.evidence: XS=8 cols, XXS=4 cols — already enforced.

items.check: Data tables and code blocks may have horizontal scroll — confirm exception is documented.
White text on Primary 500 (#0047FF) ≥ 4.5:1.
White text on Secondary 500 (#FF0405) ≥ 4.5:1.
items.evidence: Contrast ratio ≈ 3.94:1. Use Secondary 600 (#D10000, 4.97:1) or large text only.
items.remediation: Define a 'safe text on red' rule.

items.check: White text on Tertiary 500 (#5E05FF) ≥ 4.5:1.
Black text on Quaternary 500 (#FED70B) ≥ 4.5:1.
Black text on Quinary 500 (#E6FF0A) ≥ 4.5:1.
Black text on Senary 500 (#03FF17) ≥ 4.5:1.
Body text Grey 500 on Grey 0 ≥ 4.5:1.
Secondary text Grey 400 on white ≥ 4.5:1.
items.remediation: Grey 400 is for borders, dividers, placeholder only — never body text.

items.check: Border Grey 200 on white ≥ 3:1 for UI components.
items.evidence: Contrast ratio ≈ 1.65:1. OK for decorative dividers; not for form field borders.
items.remediation: Use Grey 500 (7.04:1) for any interactive border.

items.check: Semantic colours paired with icon + text label, never colour alone.
items.evidence: Token system supports it; downstream components must enforce.

items.check: Dark Grey 50 and 100 are duplicates (#1A1A1A) — confirm intentional.
items.evidence: Flagged in colours.json — verify with design team.

items.check: Font sizes use rem/em in code, not fixed px, so 200% browser zoom works.
items.evidence: Convert px tokens to rem in CSS layer (16px = 1rem base).

items.check: Default body line-height (Text Medium 24/16 = 1.5) meets text spacing minimum.
Text Small line-height (18/12 = 1.5) meets minimum.
H1 line-height (96/80 = 1.2) acceptable for display sizes.
items.evidence: Display type at 1.2 is industry standard.

items.check: Text Small (12px) is 'normal text' — needs 4.5:1 contrast.
items.evidence: Verify all 12px usages pair with Grey 500+ on light backgrounds.

items.check: Negative letter-spacing on H1 (-4%) does not impair readability.
Line length stays between 45–75 characters at body size.
items.evidence: Add max-width: 65ch to prose components downstream.

items.check: Heading hierarchy preserved in markup, not skipped for visual styling.
Cards rely on shadow alone for separation — provide a 1px border fallback for high-contrast mode.
Modal (XL elevation) backdrop must dim the page behind for focus separation.
Hover elevation lift (XS → S) is perceivable but not jarring.
items.evidence: Subtle elevation shift complies with prefers-reduced-motion.

items.check: Elevation transitions respect prefers-reduced-motion.
items.evidence: Engineers: wrap shadow transitions in @media (prefers-reduced-motion: no-preference).

items.check: Pill-shaped tokens with active states must keep focus rings visible.
Focus indicators on radius-xs (2px) inputs maintain visibility.
items.evidence: 2px outline + 2px offset = 4px halo; visible on thin inputs.

items.check: All button sizes meet 24×24 minimum target size.
Touch-priority buttons meet AAA 44×44 target.
items.evidence: M (48) and L (56) pass. XS (32) and S (40) — restrict to mouse-only contexts.

items.check: Primary: white text on #E42527 ≥ 4.5:1.
items.evidence: Contrast ratio ≈ 4.66:1 — passes AA. Tight margin.

items.check: Primary Hover: white text on #A41719 ≥ 4.5:1.
Primary Line default: #0047FF text on white ≥ 4.5:1.
Primary Line border (#0047FF, 2px) ≥ 3:1 against white.
Secondary: white text on black ≥ 4.5:1.
Tertiary: black text on white default ≥ 4.5:1.
Tertiary default has NO border — verify it's distinguishable from background context.
items.evidence: Tertiary on white surface is invisible until hovered.
items.remediation: Document: 'Tertiary requires adjacent visual cue (icon, label, or paired button).'

items.check: All buttons need a visible focus indicator. Token system does not currently define a focus-ring spec.
items.evidence: cta-buttons.json defines default/hover/active but no focus state.
items.remediation: Add 'focus' state to all 5 variants. Recommended: 2px solid #0047FF outline with 2px offset.

items.check: Buttons with icon + text need accessible name. Icon-only variants need aria-label.
Primary uses red — avoid using red as the only signal for 'destructive' meaning.
items.evidence: Naming: Primary = red, Primary Line = blue. Confirm intent.

items.check: Disabled state spec is missing from cta-buttons.json.
items.remediation: Add disabled state: 50% opacity, cursor: not-allowed, aria-disabled='true'.

items.check: State transitions respect prefers-reduced-motion.

criticalFailures.issue: White text on Secondary 500 fails AA (3.94:1).
Grey 400 on white fails AA for body text (2.85:1).
Tertiary default has no visible boundary on white surfaces.
No focus state defined in CTA token system.
No disabled state defined in CTA token system.

manual: Forced colors / High Contrast Mode test

designPhase: Run Stark contrast check on every text/background pair before handoff
Annotate focus states in Figma for every interactive element
Provide alt-text or 'decorative' tag for every image
developmentPhase: Add aria-label to every icon-only button
Run axe DevTools before merging any UI PR
qaPhase: Run automated audit (Lighthouse + axe) on every page
Manual screen reader pass on critical flows
Test at 200% zoom on Chrome and Safari
